item.description: NBC News projects incumbent Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass will advance to a November runoff election, with challengers Spencer Pratt and Nithya Raman competing for the other spot. NBC Los Angeles’ Alex Rozier reports from Bass’ campaign headquarters where she spoke ahead of the call.

media:content.media:title: Karen Bass to advance in L.A. mayoral runoff race, NBC News projects
media:content.media:description: NBC News projects incumbent Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass will advance to a November runoff election, with challengers Spencer Pratt and Nithya Raman competing for the other spot. NBC Los Angeles’ Alex Rozier reports from Bass’ campaign headquarters where she spoke ahead of the call.

item.title: Karen Bass to advance in L.A. mayoral runoff race, NBC News projects

item.description: Democrat Xavier Becerra and Republican Steven Hilton addressed supporters as voters took to the polls in California’s governor primary.

media:content.media:title: California governor candidates address supporters
media:content.media:description: Democrat Xavier Becerra and Republican Steven Hilton addressed supporters as voters took to the polls in California’s governor primary.

item.title: California governor candidates address supporters

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Allen
item.description: Rep. Randy Feenstra conceded to businessman Zach Lahn in Iowa’s Republican primary for governor as party voters rejected President Donald Trump’s late endorsement in the race.

media:content.media:text: Syndication: The Des Moines Register
media:content.media:description: Iowa Republican gubernatorial candidate Zach Lahn speaks at the Republican Party of Polk County’s Lincoln Dinner in Des Moines on March 26.

media:content.media:text: Rep. Randy Feenstra
media:content.media:description: Iowa Republican gubernatorial candidate Zach Lahn speaks at the Republican Party of Polk County’s Lincoln Dinner in Des Moines on March 26.

media:content.media:text: Iowa State Auditor Rob Sand
media:content.media:description: Iowa Republican gubernatorial candidate Zach Lahn speaks at the Republican Party of Polk County’s Lincoln Dinner in Des Moines on March 26.

item.title: MAHA-backed Zach Lahn defeats Trump-backed Randy Feenstra in Iowa GOP primary for governor

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: The ballots are set in two Republican-held battleground House races in Iowa, part of a slate of primaries Tuesday shaping the battle for control of Congress in the fall

media:content.media:text: Rep. Zach Nunn, R-Iowa, and Democratic candidate for Iowa’s 3rd Congressional District, Sarah Trone Garriott.
media:content.media:description: Republican Rep. Zach Nunn and Democratic challenger Sarah Trone Garriott will face off in Iowa's 3rd Congressional District.

media:content.media:text: Syndication: The Des Moines Register
media:content.media:description: Republican Rep. Zach Nunn and Democratic challenger Sarah Trone Garriott will face off in Iowa's 3rd Congressional District.

item.title: Voters select candidates in key House districts that could decide the majority

item.dc:creator: Henry J. Gomez
item.description: Iowa state Rep. Josh Turek has won the Democratic nomination for his state’s open U.S.

media:content.media:text: Political Candidates Crisscross Iowa To Campaign Ahead Of State Primary
media:content.media:description: Democratic Senate candidate Josh Turek during a campaign event in Westside, Iowa on May 29, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Iowa Senate Candidate Ashley Hinson Votes On Primary Day
media:content.media:description: Republican Senate candidate Rep. Ashley Hinson on June 2, 2026 in Marion, Iowa.

item.title: Josh Turek wins Democratic primary in battleground Iowa Senate race

item.dc:creator: Megan Lebowitz
item.description: Republican businessman Toby Doeden has advanced to a runoff in South Dakota governor’s race, NBC News projects.

media:content.media:text: Larry Rhoden.
media:content.media:description: South Dakota Gov. Larry Rhoden is vying for a full term after replacing Kristi Noem..

media:content.media:text: Syndication: Argus Leader
media:content.media:description: South Dakota Gov. Larry Rhoden is vying for a full term after replacing Kristi Noem..

item.title: Republican businessman Toby Doeden advances to runoff in South Dakota governor’s race

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: Republican state Rep. James Gallagher, the former state Assembly minority leader, will join Congress as its newest member after he won California’s 1st District special election Tuesday, NBC News projects.

media:content.media:text: James Gallagher speaking into a microphone at the left of the frame.
media:content.media:description: Republican state legislator James Gallagher at the California State Capitol in Sacramento last year.

item.title: Republican James Gallagher wins special election for vacant red House district in California

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
item.description: CBS News fired veteran “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley on Tuesday, a day after he confronted the show’s new executive producer at a heated staff meeting.“Your antipathy to the future of the show has come through loud and clear.

media:content.media:text: Scott Pelley
media:content.media:description: Scott Pelley, Correspondent, 60 Minutes on Sept. 25 2025.

media:content.media:text: Bari Weiss in New York in 2024.
media:content.media:description: Bari Weiss in New York in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Nick Bilton seated on stage
media:content.media:description: Nick Bilton onstage at Day 1 of the Vanity Fair New Establishment Summit 2018 at The Wallis Annenberg Center for the Performing Arts on Oct. 9, 2018.

item.title: CBS News fires ‘60 Minutes’ correspondent Scott Pelley after clash with new producer

item.dc:creator: Elmira Aliieva
item.description: Ukraine launched hundreds of drones at Russian targets early Wednesday, sending black smoke rising above the historic heart of St.

media:content.media:text: RUSSIA-POLITICS-AWARDS
media:content.media:description: In this pool photograph distributed by the Russian state agency Sputnik, Russia's President Vladimir Putin gives a speech during a state awards ceremony at the Kremlin in Moscow on May 21, 2026.

item.title: Drone attack hits Putin’s hometown as he hosts ‘Russia’s Davos’

item.description: Authorities responded to a bomb threat at a Chase Bank building in Bakersfield, California. Police say a man barricaded himself inside with several hostages. NBC News’ Steve Patterson reports.

media:content.media:title: Police respond to hostage situation inside California bank
media:content.media:description: Authorities responded to a bomb threat at a Chase Bank building in Bakersfield, California. Police say a man barricaded himself inside with several hostages. NBC News’ Steve Patterson reports.

item.title: Police respond to hostage situation inside California bank

item.description: Centcom says the U.S. military disabled the engine of a ship trying to break through the blockade of Iran's ports. NBC News' Garrett Haake reports.

media:content.media:title: U.S. targets ship trying to break through blockade
media:content.media:description: Centcom says the U.S. military disabled the engine of a ship trying to break through the blockade of Iran's ports. NBC News' Garrett Haake reports.

item.title: U.S. targets ship trying to break through blockade

item.dc:creator: David Ingram
item.description: Democrats are sharpening their attacks against Elon Musk, zeroing in on his soon-to-be-trillionaire status before his company SpaceX’s initial public offering.

media:content.media:text: Photo illustration of Elon Musk dressed in a suit jacket and hat made of $100 bills against the Earth, as seen from space.
media:content.media:description: Elon Musk is expected to become the world's first trillionaire upon SpaceX's IPO.

item.title: Elon Musk is set to become the world’s first trillionaire. Democrats are taking aim.

item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: The Supreme Court allowed Alabama to use a congressional map that eliminates one of two majority-Black districts in the state in a win for Republicans.

media:content.media:text: People hold signs that say "Black Voter Matter", "It's About Us", and "History Is Watching" while standing outside
media:content.media:description: Several pro-democracy groups protest Alabama's new congressional map in Montgomery on May 4.

item.title: Supreme Court allows Alabama to use congressional map that eliminates a majority-Black district

item.dc:creator: Dawn Liu
Erin Tan
item.description: An ad for a shepherding job went viral in China, drawing hundreds of applications from young people eager to escape the stresses of urban life.

media:content.media:text: Shepherd in north China's Inner Mongolia
media:content.media:description: Sheep being herded in China’s Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region.

item.title: No people, just sheep: The unlikely job that Chinese college grads clamored for

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Rich Schapiro
item.description: It was one of the strangest Covid fraud cases brought by the Justice Department, with the kind of wild details that seemed ripped from a Hollywood script

media:content.media:text: The Edwards family, from left, son Josh, Pastor Evan, wife Mary Jane, and daughter Joy.
media:content.media:description: The Edwards family, from left, son Josh, Pastor Evan, wife Mary Jane, and daughter Joy.

media:content.media:text: Josh Edwards is taken into custody outside the Edwards family’s New Smyrna Beach home.
media:content.media:description: Josh Edwards is taken into custody outside the Edwards family’s home in New Smyrna Beach, Fla., on Dec. 14, 2022.

media:content.media:text: The family’s New Smyrna Beach house.
media:content.media:description: The family’s New Smyrna Beach house.

item.title: Florida pastor and son’s $8 million Covid fraud case comes to bizarre end

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: Iran launched a deadly new set of attacks in the Persian Gulf on Wednesday as it traded strikes with the United States, the latest exchange to threaten the fragile ceasefire and stalled peace talks between the two countries

media:content.media:text: Debris burns in Kuwait following reports that air defences intercepted missile and drone attacks
media:content.media:description: Debris burns on the ground in a parking lot following reports of missile and drone attacks, in Sabah Al Nasser, Kuwait June 3, 2026, in this screengrab obtained from a social media video . SOCIAL MEDIA/via REUTERS THIS IMAGE HAS BEEN SUPPLIED BY A THIRD PARTY. NO RESALES. NO ARCHIVES. NEWS USE ONLY.

media:content.media:text: A satellite view of Qeshm Island in Hormozgan Province, Iran, within the Strait of Hormuz region on January 17, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Satellite imagery of Qeshm Island in Hormozgan Province, Iran.

media:content.media:text: Smoke rises from an Israeli airstrike that hit Qlaileh village, as seen from the southern port city of Tyre, Lebanon,
media:content.media:description: Smoke rises from an Israeli airstrike that hit Qlaileh village, as seen from the southern port city of Tyre, Lebanon, on Tuesday.

item.title: Iran launches deadly new strikes in Gulf as latest exchange with U.S. tests ceasefire

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: In addition to having a 7-foot-4 alien who can shoot, pass, dribble and single-handedly stop elite offenses from getting to the rim, what makes the San Antonio Spurs’ run to the NBA Finals unique is they possess what most champions typically lack: youth

media:content.media:text: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs
media:content.media:description: Victor Wembanyama of the San Antonio Spurs reacts after the Spurs defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 7 of the Western Conference finals in Oklahoma City on Saturday.

item.title: If the Spurs want to win the NBA Finals, they’ll have to buck this historical trend

item.dc:creator: Dawn Liu
Erin Tan
item.description: An ad for a shepherding job went viral in China, drawing hundreds of applications from young people eager to escape the stresses of urban life.

media:content.media:text: Shepherd in north China's Inner Mongolia
media:content.media:description: Sheep being herded in China’s Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region.

item.title: No people, just sheep: The unlikely job that Chinese college grads clamored for

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
Ben Kamisar
item.description: California’s crowded, protracted gubernatorial primary is going to take a little more time to settle

media:content.media:credit: Tom Steyer, Xavier Becerra and Steve Hilton
media:content.media:text: Tom Steyer, Xavier Becerra and Steve Hilton
media:content.media:description: Tom Steyer, Xavier Becerra and Steve Hilton.

item.title: California’s race for governor and other key primaries remain undecided

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass has advanced to a November runoff election in her bid for a second term, NBC News projects, but the opponent she’ll face in the one-on-one race remains unclear

media:content.media:text: Mayor Karen Bass
media:content.media:description: Mayor Karen Bass stands at a press conference in regards to her proposed spending plan for fiscal year 2026-27 at City Hall on Monday, April 20, 2026 in Los Angeles , Calif.

item.title: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass pulled into a runoff in her race for a second term

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: Republican state Rep. James Gallagher, the former state Assembly minority leader, will join Congress as its newest member after he won California’s 1st District special election Tuesday, NBC News projects.

media:content.media:text: James Gallagher speaking into a microphone at the left of the frame.
media:content.media:description: Republican state legislator James Gallagher at the California State Capitol in Sacramento last year.

item.title: Republican James Gallagher wins special election for vacant red House district in California
